meta.notes: Phase 1 draft business/domain knowledge, grounded only in provided project documents. | Phase 2: aligned domain vocabulary with confirmed simulator I/O.

business_idea.summary: Judge-ready Gut–Brain Simulator web app that demonstrates a computational pipeline: diet inputs → MODELED microbiome proxies → MODELED metabolite proxies → MODELED cognitive proxy outputs; benchmarked against de-identified teen pilot association charts (n=66); never used for training or tuning.
business_idea.context: Regeneron ISEF / Hudson County STEM Showcase computational biology project demo app.

evidence.snippet: Review the attachments to create brd/src pack as defined in the brd_srs_packs.zip and to improve the GUT app prompt for lovable to create this application. The created brd/src foundation documentation
Modeling Diet-Driven Microbiome and Neurotransmitter Pathways

domain_summary.summary: Gut–brain axis research suggests diet can influence cognition through intermediate biological layers: microbiome composition and microbial metabolites (e.g., SCFAs). This project builds a computational simulator to model these layers and benchmark against a separate de-identified teen pilot dataset (association charts only; no microbiome/metabolomics measured in pilot).

evidence.snippet: datasets are rarely integrated into a unified biological framework. This project uses a multi-stage  machine learning approach to explicitly model the biological sequence linking diet to cognition.  I
This approach allows for simulation of how changes in diet propagate through biological systems  and influence cognitive outcomes.
○ De-identified 2025 student dataset containing only numeric diet quality scores and  cognitive performance metrics, used solely for validation and benchmarking and  not for model training

vocabulary.definition: Bidirectional signaling network linking GI tract and CNS via neural, immune, endocrine, and metabolic pathways.

evidence.snippet: datasets are rarely integrated into a unified biological framework. This project uses a multi-stage  machine learning approach to explicitly model the biological sequence linking diet to cognition.  I

vocabulary.definition: Community of microorganisms in the human gut; represented here as modeled genus-level relative abundances (Bifidobacterium, Lactobacillus) plus Firmicutes:Bacteroidetes ratio.

evidence.snippet: ○ NHANES dietary intake datasets for nutrient intake variables  ○ USDA Food and Nutrient Database for nutrient definition standardization and  nutrient mapping

vocabulary.definition: Short-chain fatty acids (acetate, propionate, butyrate) used as modeled metabolite proxy outputs (standardized scores).

evidence.snippet: ○ NHANES dietary intake datasets for nutrient intake variables  ○ USDA Food and Nutrient Database for nutrient definition standardization and  nutrient mapping

vocabulary.definition: De-identified teen dataset with diet quality score and cognitive test metrics. Used for association benchmarking only (results charts). Never used to fit or tune simulator parameters. Has no microbiome or metabolomics measurements.

evidence.snippet: ○ De-identified 2025 student dataset containing only numeric diet quality scores and  cognitive performance metrics, used solely for validation and benchmarking and  not for model training
File contains 66 rows of pilot data with diet score and cognitive tests (Stroop, Language, Memory, Logical, Overall).

vocabulary.definition: Any training/tuning step that uses benchmark-only data (pilot dataset) or uses preprocessing fitted on full data before splitting.

evidence.snippet: not for model training  Procedure  1. Download dietary intake data from NHANES and extract nutrient intake variables

risks.risk: Overclaiming causality or diagnostic value during judging
risks.mitigation: Use simulator/hypothesis-generator language and add disclaimer.
